supply_no_matching_dose.title: Tracked supply has no matching dose
supply_no_matching_dose.description: The supply "{medication}" for {patient} does not match the medications in any dose, so it will never decrement. Check the spelling in Configure (it must match a dose's medication exactly), or remove the supply.

user.title: Add a patient
user.description: Create a medication schedule for a pet or person. Pick the patient type (for the icon) and who should be reminded; you can add their doses next.

abort.already_configured: A patient with this name is already configured.

abort.no_doses: Add a dose first. There are no doses to edit yet.
abort.no_medications: Add a dose first. Medication detail attaches to a medication already used in one of this patient's doses.
abort.no_medication_detail: Add medication detail first. There is nothing to edit yet.
abort.no_supplies: Track a supply first. There is nothing to edit yet.

error.duplicate_dose: A dose with this time and these medications already exists. Use a different time, or combine them into one dose.
error.supply_exists: This medication already has a tracked supply. Use Edit a supply to change it.

add_dose.title: Add a dose
add_dose.description: Pick the time and list the medications, then choose how it repeats: on chosen days of the week (all days = daily), every N days, or an on/off cycle, from a start date.

data.days: Days of the week (for the weekly option)
data.interval_days: Every N days (for the interval option)
data.anchor_date: Start date (for the interval or cycle option)
data.min_interval_hours: Minimum hours between doses (as-needed only; 0 = no limit)
data.max_per_day: Max doses per day (as-needed only; 0 = no limit)

data_description.meds: Separate multiple medications with &, +, or a comma. A slash only splits with spaces around it, so a name like 'Carbimazol 5mg (1/2)' stays whole.
data_description.min_interval_hours: For an as-needed medication, warn (never block) when it is logged again sooner than this many hours, e.g. a pain med no less than 4 hours apart.
data_description.max_per_day: For an as-needed medication, warn once this many doses have been logged today.

edit_dose_details.title: Edit dose
edit_dose_details.description: Change this dose and save to replace it. Editing only the schedule keeps the same entity; changing the time or medications starts a fresh entity (its history resets).

data.days: Days of the week (for the weekly option)
data.interval_days: Every N days (for the interval option)
data.anchor_date: Start date (for the interval or cycle option)
data.min_interval_hours: Minimum hours between doses (as-needed only; 0 = no limit)
data.max_per_day: Max doses per day (as-needed only; 0 = no limit)

data_description.meds: Separate multiple medications with &, +, or a comma. A slash only splits with spaces around it, so a name like 'Carbimazol 5mg (1/2)' stays whole.
data_description.min_interval_hours: For an as-needed medication, warn (never block) when it is logged again sooner than this many hours, e.g. a pain med no less than 4 hours apart.
data_description.max_per_day: For an as-needed medication, warn once this many doses have been logged today.

add_supply.title: Track a medication supply
add_supply.description: Count how much of a medication is on hand. It decrements each time a dose containing this medication is marked given, flags low at your threshold, and estimates a run-out date from the schedule. Pick the medication from your doses.

data_description.supply_refill_add: On refill, add the refill amount to what is left (e.g. 17 + 30 = 47) instead of setting the count to the refill amount.
On refill, add the refill amount to what is left (e.g. 17 + 30 = 47) instead of setting the count to the refill amount.

medication_detail.title: Medication detail
medication_detail.description: Optional reference info for a medication, kept separate from the short dose name. Pick a medication from this patient's doses; saving detail for one that already has it updates it.

data.dosage: Dosage summary (e.g. 2 tablets twice a day)
Dosage summary (e.g. 2 tablets twice a day)

settings.title: Reminder settings
settings.description: Patient type, who to notify, time display, when doses reset for the day, and how the reminders nag.

data_description.nag_interval: Use a multiple of 5; reminders are evaluated on a 5-minute tick, so other values re-nag less evenly.
